questions.question: A customer calls the Sylvi support number and presses option 2 on the IVR. Which department receives this call?

options.text: Orders
General & Customer Support
Refunds
Returns & Replacements

questions.question: A customer intended to ask about a product repair but accidentally pressed option 1 (Orders) on the IVR. The call reaches a CRM Executive. What is the correct action?

options.text: Disconnect and ask the customer to call back and re-enter the correct option
Acknowledge the misdirection, handle the query if it is within the Executive's scope, or warm-transfer to the correct agent — do not disconnect without resolution
Inform the customer that their query cannot be handled on this IVR option and ask them to visit the website
Transfer the customer back to the IVR system to re-enter their selection

questions.question: Which of the following is the correct mandatory opening greeting for an IBC call?

options.text: "Hello, how can I help you today?"
"Yes, this is Sylvi customer care — please tell me your issue."
"Hello, thank you for calling. This is [Name] from Sylvi customer support. How may I assist you today?"
"Hi, you've reached Sylvi support. Please share your order ID to begin."

questions.question: A customer calls about a delivery delay. Before looking up the order in FIMS, what must the CRM Executive do?

options.text: Log the call reason in the IBC Calls tab before opening FIMS
Ask the customer for their registered mobile number or Order ID and confirm the order before accessing any details
Look up the order using the customer's name and city to save time
Open FIMS first, then ask the customer to verify the details shown on screen

questions.question: After listening to the customer's full concern, what must the CRM Executive do before providing any resolution?

options.text: Check the order status in FIMS and begin the resolution immediately
Confirm whether a previous call on this issue was logged in the IBC Calls tab
Confirm the customer's issue aloud using a confirmation question before acting
Determine if the issue falls under warranty before saying anything to the customer

questions.question: When must the IBC Calls tab (Daily Work — CRM-2026.xlsx) be updated after each inbound call?

options.text: At the end of the day in one batch — to avoid interrupting call flow
Within 2 hours of the call, during a quieter period
Only for calls that result in a cancellation or escalation
Immediately after each call, before moving to the next one

questions.question: A CRM Executive handles an IBC call where a customer asked about a refund, but the issue could not be resolved in the same call and requires a follow-up. Which Resolution Status should be entered in the IBC Calls tab?

options.text: Resolved — the call was handled even if the issue was not closed
Not Applicable — refund issues are tracked separately and not logged in the IBC Calls tab
Follow-up
Closed

questions.question: Where do callback requests come from, and where are they captured for CRM to action?

options.text: Customers email Sylvi → captured in the Freshdesk portal under open tickets
Customers call and leave a voicemail → captured in the MyOperator missed calls log
Customers send a WhatsApp message → auto-captured in the Callback Request tab of Daily Work — CRM-2026.xlsx
Customers fill a website form → requests are forwarded to the `customer-care` Slack channel

questions.question: When reviewing the Callback Request tab, what is the correct prioritisation order for pending requests?

options.text: Oldest request date first, then newest — regardless of issue type
Complaints (wrong product, non-delivery) → Issues (return/replacement, refund delays) → General queries (order status, product info)
By agent availability — the agent with the fewest open callbacks handles next
Highest order value first, then lower value orders

questions.question: A CRM Executive calls a customer from the Callback Request tab and gets no answer. What is the correct retry approach?

options.text: Mark the customer as "Unreachable" immediately and move to the next entry
Call once more the same day; if still no answer, escalate to the Asst Manager
Attempt 2–3 times spaced across the day (morning, midday, late afternoon); after 3 failed attempts mark Comments as "Unreachable — 3 attempts"
Call twice only — the system auto-closes the entry after 2 failed attempts

questions.question: After completing a callback and resolving the customer's issue, which four fields must be updated in the Callback Request tab?

options.text: Customer Name, Resolution Status, Called On, Agent Remarks
Customer Phone Number, Called By, Comments, Request Date
Called By, Call Attempt Count, Resolution Status, FIMS Order Status
Call Back Done? (→ Yes), Called By, Called On, Comments

questions.question: What is the correct navigation path to access the Follow-Up module in MyOperator?

options.text: MyOperator → Settings → Follow Up Tasks
MyOperator → Dashboard → Pending Calls → Follow Up
MyOperator → Agents → Assigned Follow-Ups
MyOperator → Call → Follow Up

questions.question: After completing a follow-up call, which two systems must be updated?

options.text: FIMS order status + IBC Calls tab
MyOperator system follow-up status + Daily Work — CRM-2026.xlsx → Call-Followup tab
MyOperator system follow-up status + Callback Request tab
Call-Followup tab + a message to the `customer-care` Slack channel

questions.question: What must be true about the MyOperator Follow Up tab at end of day?

options.text: All entries should be in "In Progress" status — they will be completed the following morning
A minimum of 80% of entries must be actioned; the remaining are carried over
Zero open unactioned entries — all items must be either Closed or have specific remarks describing what is outstanding
The Follow Up tab is reviewed weekly by the Asst Manager — daily clearance is not required

questions.question: A customer on an IBC call becomes abusive and starts making threats. What is the correct action?

options.text: Ask the customer to calm down; if they continue, end the call without escalating — this is handled at agent level
Continue the call professionally — it is never acceptable to disconnect from a customer
Note the incident in the IBC sheet and escalate to the Asst Manager at end of day
Transfer to the Asst Manager immediately and inform via direct call or `customer-care` Slack — do not continue the call alone

questions.question: A CRM Executive resolves a customer's delivery issue on an IBC call and updates the IBC sheet with Resolution Status: "Resolved". The customer asks "So my issue is fully sorted?" — the Executive answers "Yes" based on what was discussed, without actually checking the order status in FIMS. Which documented mistake was made?

options.text: The IBC sheet was updated too early — it must only be updated at end of day
The Executive did not use the correct closing script before updating the sheet
No mistake was made — the Executive gave the customer a clear answer and updated the sheet
Marking an issue as "Resolved" without confirming with the customer that the issue is actually closed
